translated.text: Hello friends, welcome to Gate Smashers. In this video we are going to discuss what is primary key.
The concept of keys in DBMS is most important whether you talk about competitive exam,
whether you talk about college or university level exam or you talk about placement,
you also ask about the concept of keys there. And it is not like the key is only one,
means what is primary key or what is candidate key, not enough.
If you talk about normalization, if you talk about sequel, you will have the concept of keys everywhere.
So in this video I am focusing only on primary key. So what is the story of primary key?
Means what is it? How do we talk? So I am starting this story with a simple example.
Let's say we have 100 students in the class. Now we have to store the data of 100 students.
Now I will not always call 100 students by face but if I have to take attendance, it is an online system.
So what will I do in the online system? I will save all the details of all the students.
I will make an excel file in which I will make the registration number, role number, father name, mother name,
student name, age, gender, marks, address, phone number, address, multiple values,
I will make it in multiple attributes. Means we will make a table in which we will define multiple attributes.
Then we will fill all the details of all the students here. You can visualize that there is a table in which we have made all the entries.
Now here comes the concept of key. What is the meaning of key?
For example we have key in the lock, the property of it is that each lock has a unique key.
That key will only open the same lock. Let's talk about the best case. I am not talking about normal case.
In India a lot can happen that you open multiple lock with one key but I am not talking about key.
I am talking about master key which is roaming around with a thief. The concept of normal key is that
for one lock, one key is made. Yes, I can open that lock with that key. So that is what we are doing here.
Means you are identifying something uniquely. If there are two things, one is like that,
then identify them that you are different and you are different. How will you do it? With key.
Now let's talk about the database here. How will we do it in the database?
You have attributes in the database. Now I will say if we take the name of the student.
So is the student's name guaranteed that it will be different? No, you can check yourself.
There are a lot of students in your college or university whose name is there.
First name, last name also see what will be there. It will be the same. You cannot take the guarantee.
Take age, take date of birth. It is 365 days in a year or if it is 360 in a year,
then obviously somewhere or the other the date of birth will be the same.
If we talk here, father name can also be same, mother name can also be same.
In case if two brothers and brothers are together, then obviously their father name and mother name will be same.
This means multiple attributes can have same values. But which attribute whose same value
can not be the same? It will be unique. Which one can be? Let's say your phone number.
You will think that the phone number will be different. Okay? The phone number will be different whenever it is.
The address card number. You can say that yes we have the address card.
Everyone's number will be different. The pan card although I am talking about students,
in the case of student, even if the pan card is not available, if you take it then it will be unique.
If you take the license number then it will be unique. You take the registration number or the roll number
because in many universities registration number is running, in many colleges the roll number is running.
So what will be that? It will be unique. So you can count multiple attributes in this way
that all these will be unique and there can't be duplicates in them.
Now let's talk about all these attributes. We call these attributes as Candidate Key.
What are all these attributes? These are Candidate Keys. So what is the property of Candidate Keys?
It is unique. So I have achieved the unique. But will it work by itself? It can't work. How?
Let's suppose I am assuming that the phone number, if I want to find out a student,
then I will fill the phone number and details will come. So is it guaranteed that every student
has given the right phone number or even phone number? This is also not guaranteed.
And there is a case also possible that let's suppose if two brothers and sisters are together
then it is possible that both have given the same number in the database. It is possible that
this case is possible that you can say that the student has not given the phone number
or it is also possible that if there are two siblings then they have mentioned one phone number somewhere.
They don't have a different phone number. Let's say Adharkar. Now Adharkar also you can say
that it is not necessary that every student has applied. If every student has applied then obviously
its Adharkar will be unique. But is it guaranteed that if you talk about the college or university,
then let's suppose there are 10,000, 20,000 students, 50,000 students, can it be guaranteed
that all the students have Adharkar, all the students have a license number of a pan card.
This is not guaranteed. And because of this also it is possible that I am looking for a student
meaning if I have put his entry online then his corresponding two or three entries have come
or his corresponding entry has not come because he was not in your database.
So the second property here is Null. Meaning also it is possible that I have not given the phone number.
I have taken admission in a college, they asked for the phone number and I have not given it yet
or I have given a wrong number. So obviously say that I should keep this field Null.
Or if you give the same phone number then he will pick up the same phone number.
And because of that tomorrow you will find the student and his corresponding one entry will not come.
There can be two or three entries. So it has not been identified uniquely.
So the concept of the database is that I can identify each one uniquely.
I can identify each one in a double code. This will not be achieved.
So this will not be achieved. One should be unique. Second is not Null.
What is not Null means that you cannot leave this field empty.
You cannot leave this field empty. You will have to fill in this field.
Let's suppose if I talk about phone number or the card, then if you have a student that you want to take admission in your college
you will fill that field. Now if you fill that field, if the student says that I do not have the card number
then what will you do? Either you will enter a wrong value in that field like dollar, hash just to fill it.
Because you cannot leave empty. It is a not Null field. So will you enter the wrong entry in that database?
What can be the second case? You will say that you have to take admission number 2 because you will have to fill in the card number.
It is not Null field. Null means empty. If you cannot leave empty then what will you do?
You will say that you will not get admission now. First bring the card number.
So obviously this case will not happen. Obviously some competitor will pull the student from college university.
Talk about the pan card number, talk about the license number. You cannot fill this case in the mandatory field.
Who will you fill in the mandatory field? In the case of student, you will fill in the registration number or the roll number.
So what is the concept of the primary key? The primary key is unique plus it is not Null.
So I got one advantage from the candidate key. It will always be unique. But out of all the candidate key,
I will only get one which is the most appropriate. Like I have told you in the case of student,
which will be the most appropriate or registration number or roll number. If you are in a small college then you can take the roll number.
If you are in university then you will get the registration number or serial number. The primary key is there.
Because it is unique. Because it is a candidate then it is unique plus it is not Null.
Means you cannot leave it empty. So whenever you talk about this case then take any database.
In that database your primary key is unique plus it is not Null. And the second primary key value we never take from the user.
We give the primary key value to the user. So if you go to any college or university to take the admission then you will not say
that I want this roll number or this registration number or VIP registration number. Nothing like that.
They will automatically generate you that this is your registration number or roll number. Because you cannot leave this field empty.
If you go to the Passport office in Passport office what will be the passport number? The primary key will be because it is unique plus it is not Null.
In Passport office the data of the students who have passports is there. Means those who applied for passports.
Now you ask for the passport number yourself? No, they will give you the passport number that is the primary key.
If you are applying for a license in such a license office then in the license office the data of the same people who have already applied for a license or they have taken a proper license.
So the license number will also be provided by the office person because this is a very tough field.
Means it becomes tough somewhere here because it is unique and Null. You have to achieve both. The data should be unique and Null should also be there.
Means you cannot leave the field empty and you cannot fill the duplicate data in it. So this is the main concept of the primary key.
Always remember that the primary key is unique plus not Null but what is the candidate key? It is unique.
You can leave it empty and you can skip it but if you do not want to skip then what will you make it? The primary key.
So last point here that in one database more than one means 2, 3, 4 can be the primary keys. No, it will be only one primary key.
Because I have already told you that making the primary key is very hard. You cannot leave it empty or you cannot fill the duplicate.
So if you try to make more than one attributes of the primary key although your database will not allow it first.
No server, no software you are using, whether you are using Rackle, SQL Server, IBM, no one will allow you.
Because if my work is done by one person then why will I hire two people? The same work here.
When my work is done by one attribute then I will not take any trouble with multiple attributes.
So that is why your software does not allow you to make more than one primary key. Yes, more than one can be candidate keys
because it can be unique, multiple attributes, it can be single or Null. You can make more than one attribute Null too.
But if we talk about it in a combined way, it will be unique and not Null only will be one attribute and it is called primary key.
And it depends on the database that you are making in which scenario, it will be in the student's scenario,
in the case of Adharkar, in the case of Pan, in the case of Passport, in the case of License, in the case of License.
So in the database you will talk about it, it will be clear what is the concept of primary key. Thank you.
Thank you.

concepts.name: Primary Key
concepts.description: The chosen candidate key used as the main identifier for rows.

concepts.name: Database
concepts.description: An organized system for storing and retrieving structured data.

concepts.name: Not Null
concepts.description: A constraint that forbids missing values.

concepts.name: Unique Constraint
concepts.description: A rule that prevents duplicate values in one or more columns.

concepts.name: Null
concepts.description: A marker representing missing or unknown data.

concepts.name: Candidate Key
concepts.description: A minimal set of attributes that uniquely identifies each tuple.

concepts.name: SQL
concepts.description: The standard query language used to work with relational databases.

concepts.name: Normalization
concepts.description: A design process that reduces redundancy and anomalies in relational schemas.

concepts.name: Relation
concepts.description: A table-like structure in the relational model.

concepts.name: Attribute
concepts.description: A column or property in a relation.

edges.reason: Relations are foundational units inside a relational database.
Attributes are properties of a relation.
The lecture contrasts null values with not-null constraints.
A primary key is chosen from candidate keys.
Normalization operates on relations and schemas.
Primary keys matter for higher normal forms.
SQL is introduced in the context of relational databases.
